stories.description: Load List Service + Load List Screen (Sprint C Foundation)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: `loads.ts` exports 3 functions: `fetchLoads()` calling `api.get("/loads")` returning the raw array, `fetchLoadById(id)` calling `fetchLoads()` then filtering by `id`, and `updateLoadStatus(id, status)` calling `api.patch`

`load.ts` defines a `Load` interface with `id`, `status`, `pickup_date`, and a `legs` array of `LoadLeg` objects (each with `type`, `city`, `state`, `facility_name`, `date`, `sequence_order`). Also exports `getOrigin(load)` and `getDestination(load)` helpers that extract the first Pickup and last Dropoff leg's city+state
`LoadCard.tsx` renders a `Pressable` card showing origin (from `getOrigin(load)`), destination (from `getDestination(load)`), and a color-coded status badge
`loads.tsx` renders 1 `FlatList` of `LoadCard` components with `renderItem` fed by `fetchLoads()` response data array
`loads.tsx` passes 2 refresh props (`refreshing={refreshing}` and `onRefresh`) to `FlatList` that re-call `fetchLoads()`
`loads.tsx` renders `<ActivityIndicator />` when `loading` state is `true` during initial fetch
`loads.tsx` renders an error `<Text>` and a "Retry" `<Pressable>` when `fetchLoads()` returns an error
`loads/[id].tsx` calls `fetchLoadById(id)` on mount via `useEffect` and renders 1 `Load` object (fetched by filtering the full loads list since no GET /loads/:id endpoint exists)

`loads/[id].tsx` renders 4 labeled sections: origin (via `getOrigin(load)` from first Pickup leg), destination (via `getDestination(load)` from last Dropoff leg), `pickup_date`, and delivery date (from last Dropoff leg's `date` field)
`loads/[id].tsx` renders 1 status badge component with the `load.status` value mapped to a distinct background color per status
`loads/_layout.tsx` defines a `Stack` navigator with 2 screens: `index` (list) and `[id]` (detail)
`LoadCard` calls `router.push("/loads/${load.id}")` on `Pressable` press to navigate to detail
`StatusUpdateButton` accepts 2 props (`currentStatus: LoadStatus`, `onStatusChange`) and renders 1+ `Pressable` buttons for valid next statuses
`useLoadStatus` hook exports 1 function `transitionTo(status)` that calls `updateLoadStatus(loadId, status)` from `loads.ts`
`useLoadStatus` sets status optimistically before the API call and reverts to `previousStatus` in the `catch` block on error
`loads/[id].tsx` renders 1 `StatusUpdateButton` with 2 props: `currentStatus={status}` and `onStatusChange={transitionTo}`
`StatusUpdateButton` filters to 3 driver transitions: "dispatched" to "in_transit", "in_transit" to "arrived", "arrived" to "delivered"
A failed transition renders an error `<Text>` showing the server's `message` field from the 422 response body
`useLoadStatus` rejects an invalid transition by rolling back optimistic state and displaying the "Invalid load transition" 422 error

stories.description: Camera Capture + Image Preview (Sprint D Foundation)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: `package.json` declares 2 new dependencies: `"expo-camera"` and `"expo-image-manipulator"` in the `dependencies` object
`app.json` includes `"NSCameraUsageDescription"` string and Android `"CAMERA"` permission in the `plugins` or `permissions` section
`CameraScreen.tsx` imports `CameraView` from `expo-camera` and calls `takePictureAsync()` on a "Capture" `Pressable` press
`PreviewScreen.tsx` renders the photo via `<Image source={{ uri }}>` with 2 buttons: "Retake" (navigates back) and "Use Photo" (proceeds)
`imageService.ts` exports `compressImage(uri)` calling `manipulateAsync` with `resize` action (max 1920px) and `compress: 0.7`
`CameraScreen.tsx` calls `useCameraPermissions()` and renders a "Grant Permission" prompt when `status` is "denied"
`documents.ts` exports 4 functions: `uploadDocument`, `triggerOcr`, `getOcrResult`, `listDocuments`
`api.ts` exports `uploadFile(urlPath, formData)` that sends `FormData` via `fetch()` without setting "Content-Type" header
`upload.tsx` renders a picker with 5 document types: "BOL", "Rate Confirmation", "POD", "Fuel Receipt", "Scale Ticket" (matching server's supported document types)
`UploadScreen.tsx` calls `uploadDocument()` with 3 params: compressed photo `uri`, `loadId`, and selected `documentType`

`upload.tsx` calls `triggerOcr(documentId)` using the `documentId` field from the upload response (NOT `document.id`) and navigates to `ocr-result` screen. Note: server also auto-fires OCR for eligible types, so this is a redundant-but-safe explicit trigger

`ocr-result.tsx` calls `getOcrResult(documentId)` on mount and renders the server's `fields[]` array using a `FlatList` or `map()` showing each item's `field_name`, `extracted_value`, and `confidence` score
`UploadScreen.tsx` renders an error `<Text>` and a "Retry" `<Pressable>` when `uploadDocument()` returns an error
`api.ts` `uploadFile` deletes the "Content-Type" key from headers so the runtime sets the multipart boundary automatically
`UploadScreen.tsx` renders "File too large" error text when the server returns status code 413

`DocumentList.tsx` calls `listDocuments(loadId)` on mount via `useEffect` and re-fetches on screen focus using `useFocusEffect` from `expo-router` (so new documents appear after camera capture flow returns)
`DocumentList.tsx` renders each document in a `FlatList` showing 3 fields: `document_type`, `filename`, `created_at`
`loads/[id].tsx` renders a "Capture Document" `Pressable` that navigates to `CameraScreen` with `loadId` param
`DocumentList.tsx` renders "No documents yet" `<Text>` when the documents array has length 0

stories.description: Connectivity Service + Offline Banner (Sprint E Foundation)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: `package.json` declares `"@react-native-community/netinfo"` in the `dependencies` object
`connectivity.ts` calls `NetInfo.addEventListener` and exports 1 boolean `isOnline` tracking connectivity state changes
`ConnectivityContext.tsx` exports 2 items: `ConnectivityProvider` component and `useConnectivity` hook returning `{ isOnline: boolean }`
`OfflineBanner.tsx` calls `useConnectivity()` and renders "You are offline" `<Text>` only when `isOnline === false`
Root `_layout.tsx` wraps app tree with 1 `<ConnectivityProvider>` wrapper and renders 1 `<OfflineBanner />` at top level
`package.json` declares both `"expo-file-system"` and `"@react-native-async-storage/async-storage"` in the `dependencies` object
`fileStorage.ts` exports 2 functions: `saveFileLocally(uri)` returning the permanent path and `deleteLocalFile(path)` removing the file
`uploadQueue.ts` exports 3 functions: `addToQueue()`, `processQueue()`, and `getQueueItems()`
`uploadQueue.ts` computes retry delay as `Math.pow(2, retryCount) * 1000` ms (exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s)
`queue.ts` defines `QueueItem` with 7 fields: `id`, `filePath`, `loadId`, `documentType`, `status`, `retryCount`, `createdAt`
`uploadQueue.ts` calls `AsyncStorage.setItem("uploadQueue", JSON.stringify(items))` to persist queue state
`fileStorage.ts` calls `FileSystem.copyAsync` with 2 params `{ from: uri, to: permanentPath }` to save files from temp storage
`processQueue()` marks items with `retryCount >= 5` as `status: "failed"` permanently and skips them
`package.json` declares `"expo-task-manager"` in the `dependencies` object
`backgroundSync.ts` calls `TaskManager.defineTask("loadpilot-upload-sync", handler)` with a handler that runs `processQueue()`
The background task handler calls 1 function `processQueue()` and returns `BackgroundFetch.BackgroundFetchResult.NewData`
`connectivity.ts` calls `processQueue()` when `NetInfo` transitions from `isConnected === false` to `isConnected === true`
Root `_layout.tsx` calls `registerBackgroundSync()` inside 1 `useEffect` hook with empty dependency array `[]` during mount

stories.description: Queue Status UI + Offline Upload Integration

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: `QueueStatusBadge.tsx` calls `getQueueItems()` and renders a red badge with the count of items where `status === "pending"` or `status === "failed"`
`QueueScreen.tsx` renders all queue items in a `FlatList` showing 4 fields: filename, `documentType`, `status`, `retryCount`
`QueueScreen.tsx` renders a "Retry" `<Pressable>` for each item with `status === "failed"` that re-triggers upload

`upload.tsx` checks `useConnectivity().isOnline`; when `isOnline === false`, calls `saveFileLocally()` then `addToQueue()` instead of direct upload; when online upload fails with a network error, falls back to the same queue path
Tab `_layout.tsx` adds a 4th `Tabs.Screen` for `queue` with `QueueStatusBadge` rendering the pending count as a tab badge number (visible when count > 0)
`index.tsx` calls `fetchLoads()` and renders an "Active Loads" card counting loads with status in `["dispatched", "in_transit", "arrived"]`
`index.tsx` calls `getQueueItems()` and renders a "Pending Uploads" card showing the count when count > 0
`scripts/verify-sprint-cde.cjs` exists and invokes all 10 phase `verify-*.cjs` scripts, reporting aggregated pass/fail totals
`scripts/verify-sprint-cde.cjs` calls `process.exit(1)` when `failures > 0` and `process.exit(0)` when all checks pass
